text.title: Product Analytics Core Rules
text.summary: Tracking-plan + funnel/cohort instrumentation discipline (AARRR / North Star) feeding agent-readable BI sources for activation diagnosis, weekly health digests, and experiment readouts.

rule.statement: Every product MUST maintain a tracking plan (YAML/JSON) under version control: event_name, properties, owner, status (live/deprecated). Untracked events emitted from code are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Untracked events break trend lines on first deploy.

rule.statement: Event names MUST follow `object_action` (snake_case), past tense. Free-form names produce duplicates that fragment funnels.
rule.rationale: Naming chaos is the #1 cause of broken funnels in mature instrumentations.

rule.statement: Every product MUST publish a North Star + 2-3 input metrics with explicit causal-link diagrams. Vanity metrics in dashboards without input chain are forbidden.

rationale.source: Sean Ellis / Andrew Chen North Star Metric

rule.rationale: Without a metric tree, all metrics look equally important.

rule.statement: The agent-readable health digest MUST cite explicit anomaly rules (e.g., 'any cohort retention WoW change > 3pp', 'funnel step conversion ±10% absolute').
rule.rationale: Soft-judgment anomalies vary by run and produce inconsistent alerts.

rule.statement: Event properties containing PII MUST be redacted before being fed to a remote LLM. Redaction happens at ingest, not at synthesis.

If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — pre-MVP without live events; product fully measured by external vendor with no in-app behaviour; compliance lockdown), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
